---
title: "APEX: Autonomous Predictive Equity eXperiment"
subtitle: "A 30-Day Empirical Study of LLM-Augmented Algorithmic Trading"
author: "Lee Israel"
date: "2026-05-08"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry: margin=1in
    fontsize: 11pt
bibliography: references.bib
---

## Abstract

This paper presents APEX (Autonomous Predictive Equity eXperiment), a 30-day empirical study evaluating the performance of a Claude-powered autonomous trading agent on US equities. The agent combines cross-sectional momentum and mean-reversion strategies with LLM-based decision reasoning. Operating on Alpaca Markets from April 1–30, 2026 — first in paper trading mode, then with a $150 live capital cap — APEX generates, evaluates, and executes signals without human intervention. We report risk-adjusted returns, decision quality metrics, and qualitative analysis of the agent's reasoning chain. All code, data, and decision logs are open-sourced.

**Keywords**: algorithmic trading, large language models, autonomous agents, momentum strategy, mean reversion

---

## 1. Introduction

*[To be written during experiment — document motivation, prior work, research questions]*

---

## 2. Methodology

### 2.1 System Architecture

APEX consists of three components: a signal generation layer (vectorbt backtested strategies), a reasoning layer (Claude API), and an execution layer (Alpaca Markets). The agent runs on a fixed schedule: 09:31 ET for morning signals, 15:45 ET for afternoon rebalance, and 16:05 ET for end-of-day analysis.

### 2.2 Strategies

**Momentum**: Cross-sectional 20-day risk-adjusted momentum. Long top-5 ranked equities from the S&P 100 universe.

**Mean Reversion**: Bollinger Band (20-day, 2σ) reversion. Long when price falls 2+ standard deviations below the 20-day mean.

### 2.3 Risk Management

- Maximum 10 concurrent positions
- Maximum 5% portfolio weight per position
- 2% trailing stop-loss on all positions
- Hard live capital cap: $150

---

## 3. Strategy Backtests

*[Populated during development — insert vectorbt results below]*

```{python}
#| label: backtest-results
#| eval: false
# Results from src/backtest/run.py
# Insert momentum and mean-reversion backtest stats here
```

---

## 4. Experiment Results

*[Populated during April experiment]*

---

## 5. Discussion

*[Populated post-experiment]*

---

## 6. Conclusion

*[Populated post-experiment]*

---

## References

::: {#refs}
:::
